---
title: ""
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero-card}

# Tracing the Embargo Breach

## Visual Analytics for VAST Challenge 2026 Mini-Challenge 1

TenantThread's merger announcement was supposed to remain under embargo until **6:00 PM on 5 June 2046**. However, merger-related information appeared publicly on FleX before the official release time.

This project uses visual analytics to investigate how the breach happened, who was involved, how communication behaviour changed, and whether the release was a planned leak or a system breakdown under pressure.

:::

## Project Background
TenantThread is a property technology company involved in a sensitive merger with CivicLoom Realty Partners. The deal, codenamed **Project HarborCrest**, was under strict information embargo. At the same time, TenantThread was facing rising public pressure over its **Retention Optimizer** product and growing criticism from SaltWind Journal and social media users.

An automated compliance tool known as **The Judge** was introduced to prevent embargo breaches. However, during the crisis, embargoed merger information still appeared publicly before the official release time.

This project investigates the communication records from the two weeks leading up to the breach to understand what happened.

## Investigation Focus

The central question of this project is:

>> Did TenantThread deliberately leak the merger, or did the system break down under pressure?

The analysis focuses on three areas:

1. **Key events and relationships** that led to the inappropriate release.
2. **Behavioural changes** between normal communication and crisis-period communication.
3. **Leading indicators** that showed the release was possible before it happened.

## Analytical Approach

This project applies a structured visual analytics workflow to reconstruct the breach.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[Raw MC1 JSON Data] --> B[Data Preparation]
  B --> C[Event Timeline]
  B --> D[Actor-Channel Analysis]
  B --> E[Sensitive Language Tracking]
  C --> F[Embargo Breach Sequence]
  D --> F
  E --> F
  F --> G[Leading Indicators]
  G --> H[Final Interpretation]
```

The analysis begins by transforming the nested MC1 JSON data into clean round-level and communication-level tables. These tables are then used to build visualisations that show how the breach developed over time.

## Key Visual Analytics Components

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

### Event Timeline
The timeline reconstructs the main sequence of events leading to the premature release. It highlights important triggers, decision points and public communication moments.

### Actor-Channel Heatmap
The heatmap shows how actors used different channels across the crisis period. This helps reveal shifts from routine communication to side-channel and public-facing activity.

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

### Sensitive Language Tracker
This view tracks deal-related, embargo-related and crisis-related terms over time. It helps identify when communication became more risky.

### Leading Indicators View
This view identifies early warning signs, such as side-channel coordination, continued sensitive discussion after warnings, and public signalling before the official release.

:::

::::

## Preliminary Finding
The preliminary finding is that the breach was **not a planned leak by one individual from the start**. Instead, it appears to be a **system breakdown under pressure**.

As the crisis intensified, TenantThread's communication behaviour changed. The team moved from routine checking to urgent story control. After SaltWind's 5:00 PM story, Legal treated the embargo as already overtaken by events and gave the **“GO” at 5:19 PM**. This led to merger-related posts being released before the official **6:00 PM** embargo lift.

## Expected Outcome 
The expected outcome is an interactive visual analytics system that allows users to:

- trace the full release pathway
- compare normal and abnormal communication behaviour
- identify the actors, channels and decisions involved
- detect warning signs before the breach, and
- assess whether the breach was deliberate or caused by system failure under pressure.

The final project will combine timeline analysis, behavioural comparison and evidence-based interpretation to support CivicLoom's legal investigation.

## Project Sections

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="33%"}

### Proposal
The proposal explains the motivation, objectives, data, methodology, prototype sketches, R packages and project schedule.

[View Proposal](proposal.html)

:::

::: {.column width="33%"}

### Methodology
The methodology section documents the data preparation, wrangling logic, derived variables and visual analytics design.

[View Methodology](methodology.html)

:::

::: {.column width="33%"}

### Findings
The findings section presents the final visual analysis and interpretation of the embargo breach.

[View Findings](findings.html)

:::

::::
